observation: 23/84 tasks fail. Five tasks hit the step limit without finishing (dna-assembly @step34, sqlite-with-gcov @step19, build-pmars @step26, extract-elf @step50, mteb-retrieve @step20) — these have modest token counts, meaning the agent was doing real work but ran out of budget. The current _COMMANDS_DESC only says 'the array can be empty if you want to wait'; it gives no guidance to batch multiple related commands into a single execute_commands call. Since each call consumes one step, an agent that runs ./configure, then make, then make test in three separate calls burns 3 steps where 1 would suffice. Additionally, make-mips-interpreter fails with 'nul byte found in provided data' — a hard fatal error caused by the agent embedding \x00 in a keystroke string; the current _KEYSTROKES_DESC has no warning against this.
hypothesis: Explicitly instructing the agent (1) that each execute_commands call costs one step and that batching related commands reduces step burn, and (2) that null bytes in keystrokes cause a fatal internal error, will let step-starved tasks complete within budget and prevent the null-byte crash. These are purely additive description changes with no structural risk.
change_summary: Three additive description changes: (1) _EXECUTE_COMMANDS_DESC now states each call costs one step and encourages batching; (2) _COMMANDS_DESC explicitly instructs the agent to group related commands (build+test, install+run) into a single call rather than separate calls, and to chain fast sequential steps with && or ;; (3) _KEYSTROKES_DESC adds a warning that null bytes (\x00) cause a fatal internal error. No structural changes to tool schemas.
